title: Method and apparatus for determining whether to transmit packet containing a full header information or a compressed header information in wireless communication system
abstract: The present invention relates to a method of transmitting packets by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, wherein the UE is related to a first network and a second network. In particular, the method includes the steps of: receiving, from the first network, a handover command to connect with the second network; establishing a connection with the second network; transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network; and based on a connection with the first network being released, transmitting packets containing the full header information or a compressed header information to the second network.
claims: Claims ( 14 ) A method for transmitting packets by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, wherein the UE is related to a first network and a second network, the method comprising: receiving, from the first network, a handover command to connect with the second network; establishing a connection with the second network; transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network; and based on a connection with the first network being released, transmitting packets containing the full header information or a compressed header information to the second network. The method according to claim 1, wherein transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network comprises: retransmitting packets, which were not successfully transmitted to the first network, to the second network. The method according to claim 1, further comprising: configuring a radio bearer comprising one PDCP entity, at least two RLC entities, and at least two MAC entities, wherein the at least two MAC entities include a first MAC entity related to the first network and a second MAC entity related to the second network, and the at least two RLC entities include a first RLC entity related to the first MAC entity and a second RLC entity related to the second MAC entity. The method according to claim 3, further comprising: based on establishing a connection with the second network, releasing the connection with the first network, wherein releasing the connection with the first network comprises: releasing the first RLC entity and the first MAC entity. The method according to claim 1, wherein, the compressed header information is generated based on a compression algorithm related to at least one of Ethernet header compression, robust header compression (ROHC), or uplink data compression (UDC). A user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, wherein the UE is related to a first network and a second network, the UE comprising: at least one transceiver; at least one processor; and at least one computer memory operably connectable to the at least one processor and storing instructions that, when executed, cause the at least one processor to perform operations comprising: receiving, from the first network, a handover command to connect with the second network; establishing a connection with the second network; transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network; and based on a connection with the first network being released, transmitting packets containing the full header information or a compressed header information to the second network. The UE according to claim 6, wherein transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network comprises: retransmitting packets, which were not successfully transmitted to the first network, to the second network. The UE according to claim 6, wherein the operations further comprise: configuring a radio bearer comprising one PDCP entity, at least two RLC entities, and at least two MAC entities, wherein the at least two MAC entities include a first MAC entity related to the first network and a second MAC entity related to the second network, and the at least two RLC entities include a first RLC entity related to the first MAC entity and a second RLC entity related to the second MAC entity. The UE according to claim 8, wherein the operations further comprise: based on establishing a connection with the second network, releasing the connection with the first network, wherein releasing the connection with the first network comprises: releasing the first RLC entity and the first MAC entity. The UE according to claim 6, wherein, the compressed header information is generated based on a compression algorithm related to at least one of Ethernet header compression, robust header compression (ROHC), or uplink data compression (UDC). An apparatus for a user equipment (UE), wherein the UE is related to a first network and a second network, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and at least one computer memory operably connectable to the at least one processor and storing instructions that, when executed, cause the at least one processor to perform operations comprising: receiving, from the first network, a handover command to connect with the second network; establishing a connection with the second network; transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network; and based on a connection with the first network being released, transmitting packets containing the full header information or a compressed header information to the second network. A computer readable storage medium storing at least one computer program comprising instructions that, when executed by at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to perform operations for a user equipment (UE), wherein the UE is related to a first network and a second network, the operations comprising: receiving, from the first network, a handover command to connect with the second network; establishing a connection with the second network; transmitting packets containing a full header information to at least one of the first network and the second network; and based on a connection with the first network being released, transmitting packets containing the full header information or a compressed header information to the second network. A method for receiving packets by a by a first network in a wireless communication system, wherein the first network is related to a user equipment (UE), the method comprising: transmitting, to the UE, a handover command to connect with the second network; receiving packets containing a full header information from the UE; and based on a connection with the UE being released, stop transmitting or receiving packets with the UE. A first network in a wireless communication system, wherein the first network is related to a user equipment (UE), the first network comprising: at least one transceiver; at least one processor; and at least one computer memory operably connectable to the at least one processor and storing instructions that, when executed, cause the at least one processor to perform operations comprising: transmitting, to the UE, a handover command to connect with the second network; receiving packets containing a full header information from the UE; and based on a connection with the UE being released, stop transmitting or receiving packets with the UE.
